description: Scanner (market scanner) feature - new /scanner page + /api/scanner/run endpoint allowing users to batch-score a list of tickers across all 18 masters, displayed as a heatmap table
steps: 1. In dashboard/app.py: (a) Add GET /scanner route that renders scanner.html template. (b) Add POST /api/scanner/run endpoint that accepts JSON body {tickers: [...], preset?: string}. It should run get_coordinator().analyze_with_all() for each ticker (using ThreadPoolExecutor for parallelism) and return a matrix: {tickers: [{ticker, agents: [{agent_id, signal, score}]}]}. Include preset lists: 'tech_giants' = ['AAPL','NVDA','MSFT','GOOGL','TSLA','META','AMZN','AMD'], 'china_stocks' = ['BABA','PDD','JD','BIDU','NIO','LI','XPEV','00700.HK'], 'crypto' = ['BTC-USD','ETH-USD','SOL-USD','DOGE-USD'].

2. Create dashboard/templates/scanner.html extending base.html: (a) Preset buttons (Tech Giants, China Stocks, Crypto) + custom input textarea for comma-separated tickers. (b) Run button that POSTs to /api/scanner/run. (c) Results displayed as a heatmap table: rows = tickers, columns = 18 agents (abbreviated names), cells colored green/yellow/red based on signal (bullish/neutral/bearish) with score number. (d) Summary row showing consensus signal per ticker. (e) Loading skeleton during analysis.
3. In dashboard/templates/base.html sidebar: Add Scanner nav item under the 'Analysis' group, between 'Signals' and 'Backtest'. Use a grid/scan icon SVG.
4. Ensure the scanner page works with the i18n system (add data-i18n attributes to static text, add scanner translations to i18n.js).
acceptance_criteria: GET /scanner returns HTML page with preset buttons and custom input
POST /api/scanner/run with {tickers: ['AAPL','NVDA']} returns matrix of signals
Heatmap table renders with colored cells (green=bullish, yellow=neutral, red=bearish)
Preset buttons populate ticker list correctly
Scanner nav item appears in sidebar
verification: python -c "from dashboard.app import app; print('scanner route OK')"
findings: All 448 tests pass. Scanner page, API endpoint, nav item, and i18n translations all implemented successfully.
